_meta.description: World Cup 2026 match previews. 1-2 sentence editorial blurbs designed for venue cards, match alerts, and SEO landing pages. Schema matches /data/matches/2026.json from Codex Phase 1.5. Matches without confirmed fixtures use tbd:true.
_meta.tone: Fan-first, scannable, no marketing fluff. Every line should make a user want to find a room.
_meta.cardinality: 104 matches total in WC 2026; this file covers all matches surfaced in the current app + popular crossings. Codex Stream A3 fills in the rest after the official schedule is scraped.

matches.blurb_short: The opening match. Azteca on a Thursday afternoon — wear green.
matches.blurb_long: Tournament opens at the Azteca, the only stadium to host three World Cups. Mexico fans take this one personally. Expect packed rooms across CDMX, GDL, MTY, and every Mexican-American bar in LA, Houston, Dallas, and Chicago.

matches.blurb_short: 3am kickoff in Guadalajara — a global late-night for Korean and Czech bars.
matches.blurb_long: Off-hour fixture that rewards Korean barbecue spots and Czech beer halls staying open. Korean diaspora bars in LA, NY, and Toronto will be the scene.

matches.blurb_short: Canada's home opener. Toronto and Vancouver should be loud.
matches.blurb_long: First Canada match of the tournament. BMO crowd, plus a Bosnian-Canadian community in Toronto that will fill its usual rooms. Watch for the Etobicoke and Mississauga supporter scenes.

matches.blurb_short: USA's tournament opens at SoFi. Friday night, full national attention.
matches.blurb_long: Friday-night kickoff at SoFi for the USA's first match. Every USA bar across all 17 host cities will be packed. Paraguayan-American hubs in LA and NJ will hold their own corners.

matches.blurb_short: Saturday afternoon at Levi's. Quiet match for many; sharp for diaspora rooms.
matches.blurb_long: Mid-afternoon, mid-stakes for casual fans. Qatari and Swiss communities in the Bay area will pick spots; for everyone else this is a warm-up to the marquee Saturday matches.

matches.blurb_short: Brazil at MetLife. Saturday primetime. The first must-be-in-the-right-room match.
matches.blurb_long: Brazil's tournament opener in the New York metro, with Morocco's huge North African following turning every Moroccan and Brazilian bar in NYC, NJ, Boston, and Toronto into a packed house. Book early.

matches.blurb_short: Boston hosts a fascinating diaspora double-header crowd.
matches.blurb_long: Massive Haitian community across Boston, Brockton, Mattapan; massive Scottish-American pub scene throughout New England. Two completely different rooms in the same city, both loud.

matches.blurb_short: Sunday afternoon in Dallas. Izakayas and Dutch beer halls everywhere.
matches.blurb_long: Japan-Netherlands always draws — both have global supporter bases. In Dallas, look for Japanese spots in Plano and Carrollton; in NYC, izakayas will be standing-room. Dutch bars are quieter but tighter-knit.

matches.blurb_short: Late-night Vancouver kickoff. Turkish coffee and Aussie pub overlap.
matches.blurb_long: Off-hour fixture that lands midnight Pacific. Turkish cafes in Vancouver and Toronto will be open; Aussie expat pubs in LA, SF, and NYC pick up the slack.

matches.blurb_short: Philly Sunday-evening match. Watch for the Ecuadorian crowd in NJ and Queens.
matches.blurb_long: Cote d'Ivoire travels well; the Ecuadorian-American scene in Newark, Queens, and Philadelphia is a known force. Both communities pick specific bars and stay loyal — find them and they'll be packed.

matches.blurb_short: Germany's tournament opener. Beer halls everywhere will be early-Sunday loud.
matches.blurb_long: Sunday lunchtime kickoff makes this a beer-hall, brunch-with-beer kind of match. Houston has German halls; NYC's Radegast and Loreley will fill from open. Curacao has a small but loud diaspora community in NY-NJ.

matches.blurb_short: Belgium-Egypt in Seattle on a Monday afternoon.
matches.blurb_long: Weekday afternoon kickoff that rewards remote-work fans. Egyptian community in NYC's Astoria and parts of New Jersey will be in their usual rooms. Belgian bars are rare but loyal — find one and it's tight-knit.

matches.blurb_short: Miami evening. Uruguayan rooms in Miami and NJ go all-in.
matches.blurb_long: Uruguay rooms in South Florida and Hudson County NJ are old-school — Argentine-Uruguayan steakhouses with the match on every wall. Saudi following thinner stateside; Miami's Saudi-international crowd will pick a few spots.

matches.blurb_short: Spain at noon in Atlanta. Tapas bars get an early kick.
matches.blurb_long: Lunch kickoff. Spain has standout rooms in NYC (Boqueria), DC, and the Bay; Cabo Verde's Boston-area Cape Verdean community in Brockton and Dorchester is a tight scene that will pick its spots.

matches.blurb_short: Persian LA brings the volume Monday night.
matches.blurb_long: Tehrangeles is the room — Westwood, Beverly Hills Persian restaurants and bars. NYC's Persepolis and Shiraz Kitchen will fill. New Zealand has a small US following but rugby pubs typically host Kiwi matches too.

matches.blurb_short: France-Senegal at MetLife. Two of the largest fan rooms in the city collide.
matches.blurb_long: Massive French-American and Senegalese-American communities in NYC. Le Bain and Brasserie Cognac on the French side, Harlem and the Bronx Senegalese restaurants on the other. Bars that handle both crowds well book up days in advance.

matches.blurb_short: Iraq qualifies after years out. Iraqi community will be loud.
matches.blurb_long: Iraqi community in Detroit, Boston, and the LA suburbs has waited a long time. Norwegian community in the Twin Cities and parts of Brooklyn will pick its spots — Norwegian Lutheran clubs become unexpected venues.

matches.blurb_short: Argentina at Arrowhead. Tuesday night primetime.
matches.blurb_long: Argentine bars across NYC (Palermo, Parrilla), Miami, and KC will be loud and likely sold out for any Argentina match. Algerian community is tight-knit in NYC and Detroit; will find their rooms.

matches.blurb_short: Colombia in Mexico City. Latin American party for Colombian fans.
matches.blurb_long: Colombia has huge supporter scenes across Mexico City, Miami, NYC's Jackson Heights, and Houston. Uzbek community is small in NA but enthusiastic — central Asian restaurants in Brooklyn and Sacramento become event venues.

matches.blurb_short: England-Croatia repeats a decade of drama. Pubs everywhere.
matches.blurb_long: England fans pack any pub showing the match — Boston, NYC's Jones Wood Foundry, Toronto, every UK-style pub coast to coast. Croatian community in Toronto, Pittsburgh, and Cleveland holds tight, supporter-club style.

matches.blurb_short: Late-night Bay Area kickoff. Niche but loyal rooms.
matches.blurb_long: Off-hour fixture. Levantine cuisine spots in NYC (Levantine), Detroit, and SF Bay area host Jordanian fans. Austrian community is small; Cafe Katja and similar German-Austrian spots will be the home base.

matches.blurb_short: Ghana fills Toronto. Panamanian crowd in NYC and Miami too.
matches.blurb_long: Ghanaian-Canadian community in Toronto and Brampton brings real volume. Panamanian community is concentrated in NYC's Brooklyn and Miami's Allapattah — small but well-organized for matches.

matches.blurb_short: Portugal afternoon match. Newark, Toronto, and East Bay light up.
matches.blurb_long: Portuguese diaspora is enormous in Newark/Ironbound, Toronto, Boston, and the East Bay. Portuguese restaurants and supporter clubs all show every Portugal match. Congolese community concentrated in DC, Atlanta, and Houston.

matches.blurb_short: Lunch kickoff in Atlanta.
matches.blurb_long: Czech beer halls (Bohemian Hall in NYC, anything Pilsner-led) for Czechia. South African community is small but very tight in Atlanta, NYC, and parts of LA.

matches.blurb_short: Canada's second match. Vancouver primetime.
matches.blurb_local: BC Place full and Vancouver bars packed.
matches.blurb_long: Canada's second group match — energy is highest if Canada wins the first. Vancouver, Toronto, and any Canadian-themed pub will be loud. Qatari international community thin stateside.

matches.blurb_short: Mexico-Korea in Guadalajara. Marquee Mexican primetime.
matches.blurb_long: Mexico's second match. GDL packed. K-town spots in LA, NY's Koreatown, Atlanta and Houston Korean BBQ joints will pull a Korean crowd. Cantinas everywhere will show Mexico.

matches.blurb_short: USA's second match in Seattle. Pacific Northwest holiday for fans.
matches.blurb_long: Friday afternoon kickoff (USA's second match). USA bars across all 17 host cities will be packed; Australian expat pubs in LA, NYC, and SF pick up Aussie supporters.

matches.blurb_short: Brazil-Haiti, Friday night Philly. Two diasporas meet.
matches.blurb_long: Friday primetime. Brazil's NYC, Philadelphia, Boston, Newark spots full. Haitian community in Brockton, Mattapan, Brooklyn, Miami picks bars. Both fan bases drumming.

matches.blurb_short: Saturday night in KC. Quiet match for most, big for diaspora rooms.
matches.blurb_long: Ecuadorian community in Newark, Queens, and the Florida panhandle has its rooms. Curacao's smaller US footprint concentrates in NJ, NY, and South Florida.

matches.blurb_short: Uruguayan steakhouses fill across South Florida and NJ.
matches.blurb_long: Uruguay has loyal supporter rooms — Argentine-Uruguayan steakhouses, parrillas. Cabo Verde's Boston-area community is the most concentrated Cape Verdean diaspora in the world; Brockton and Dorchester are the rooms.

matches.blurb_short: Norway's marquee group match. Senegalese-American Harlem packed.
matches.blurb_long: Norway has thin US following but Norwegian-Lutheran clubs in Brooklyn and the Twin Cities show. Senegalese community in Harlem and the Bronx, plus Boston, hosts in cultural restaurants and bars.

matches.blurb_short: Germany's group decider at MetLife. Beer halls everywhere.
matches.blurb_long: Likely a group-stage decider for Germany. Radegast Hall, Loreley, Bierhaus, Heidelberg in NYC; Sauerkraut Haus in LA; every German-American beer hall in the country full and rowdy. Ecuadorian crowd in Queens and Newark holds its corners.

matches.blurb_short: England's group decider in NYC. UK pubs everywhere.
matches.blurb_long: If England's qualified for knockouts, this is the celebration room. NYC's Jones Wood Foundry, Smithfield, Banter; Boston's UK pubs; LA's Cock'n Bull. Panama in Brooklyn and Miami.

matches.blurb_short: Round of 32 at MetLife. Whichever group winners land here, NYC's biggest watch night of the tournament.
matches.blurb_long: First knockout round expansion in WC history (104-match format adds a Round of 32). Bars selling reservations weeks in advance. Watch Tonight digest the morning of.

matches.blurb_short: Round of 16 at MetLife. Sharper teams, smaller rooms, harder reservations.
matches.blurb_long: By Round of 16, casual fans drop and supporters stay. Whichever nations land here, expect their loyalists to claim specific rooms early. Reservation requests should go out 7+ days ahead.

matches.blurb_short: Quarterfinal at MetLife. Tournament-defining stakes.
matches.blurb_long: Quarterfinals split the field. Whichever two nations land here, every supporter bar in NYC, NJ, Boston, and Toronto for those countries hits standing room. Reserve early or commit to standing.

matches.blurb_short: The Final. MetLife. Sunday afternoon. Every venue in our network sold out.
matches.blurb_long: World Cup 2026 Final at MetLife Stadium, Sunday afternoon. Every bar with the right country tag will be sold out. Reservations should be booked the day after the semifinals. Expect record-setting global viewership and a complete shutdown of normal nightlife in any city with a finalist's diaspora.

_template_for_remaining_matches.kickoff_local: Human-readable local time at host stadium
_template_for_remaining_matches.round: Group Stage | Round of 32 | Round of 16 | Quarterfinal | Semifinal | Third Place | Final
_template_for_remaining_matches.blurb_short: 1 sentence, 12-18 words. Goes on the venue card and match alert subject.
_template_for_remaining_matches.blurb_long: 2-3 sentences. Where the diaspora is, what the room feel will be, any rivalry or storyline. Goes on the match landing page.
_template_for_remaining_matches.marquee: true if a Hot Spot match for the city; false otherwise
tags: array of short slugs for filtering and SEO

_writing_notes.voice: Fan-first, scannable. No marketing fluff, no generic 'iconic clash' filler. Every blurb should answer 'where will the right room be?'
_writing_notes.diaspora_anchors: Always name actual neighborhoods if possible (Brockton, Astoria, Tehrangeles, Ironbound, Jackson Heights). Specificity is the point.
